definition:
Takes random screenshots of the candidate's screen during the test.
- Interval is typically {interval}.
- Captured images are saved to the remote server.
how_it_works:
The browser automation uses WebRTC or canvas snapshotting to record the viewport.
- Bypasses traditional print screen restrictions.
faq:
Q1: How often are screenshots taken?
It happens randomly but averages the interval you set.
Q2: Does it capture external monitors?
It captures the entire visible extended desktop area.

definition: Prevents the candidate from seeing their final score immediately after submission.
how_it_works: The portal suppresses the results page redirect and instead shows a 'Thank You' message. It requires manual admin release later.
faq:
Q1: Can candidates see subsection scores?
No, all scoring is completely hidden.

definition:
Automatically trims any spaces at the beginning or end of a candidate's text answer.
- Helps prevent false negative grading on short-answer questions.
how_it_works: The grading script applies a string `.trim()` function before running regex or exact-match comparisons.
